titleStmt.title: SII 17.531: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 531. (A.R. No. 489 of 1904.) VĒDĀRAṆYAM, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.

summary.p: In the second gōpura (left of the entrance) in the Vēdāraṇyēśvara temple.
The details of date viz., Śaka 1386, Tāraṇa, Tai, 10 correspond to 1465 A.D., January 5.

This records the sale of 5 vēli of land for 200 paṇam by the Ādichaṇḍēśvara-dēvakaṉmis of the Tirumaṟaikkāḍu temple to Vēdam Ōdiṉār Puliyūr-nāṭṭu Vēḷār, who made a gift of it to God Tambirāṉār of the same temple for conducting the Śivarātri festival.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmanmakāmaṇṭalicuraṉ Ariyairāyavipāṭaṉ prabhutevarāyamakārāyar piṟutuvi Irācciyam paṇṇiyaruḷāṉiṉṟa cakāttam
1000 3 100 8 10 6ṉ mel cellāniṉṟa tāraṇa varuṣam taimātam 10 2 vetam Otiṉār puliyūr nāṭṭu veḷāṟku tirumaṟaikkāṭukoyil
Āticaṇṭecura tevarkaṉmikaḷom nilavilai piṟamāṇam paṇṇik kuṭuttapaṭi tirumaṟaikkā ṭuṭaiya tampirāṉāṟku civarā
ttiṟit tipatam tammuṭaiya ka naṭantu potukaiyil Intat tipatattukkum meṉac ce Amutupaṭi kaṟi
yamutukkum maṟṟum Uṇṭāṉatukkum Uṭalākakkoṇṭu cantirātittavaraiyum naṭatta kāṇiyāṉa
(ṉa) paṅkum vilavilaiRead ‘nilavilai’•niṟṇayamRead nirṇayam.paṇṇiṉa nilam Āvatu nattattukkum Āṟṟukkum naṭuppaṭṭa kaṭṭaḷai Oṉṟil �5- li nilam Ayvelik
kum vilai niṉṟayamRead nirṇayam.paṇṇiṉa � [2 100] Ippaṇam Irunūṟum paṟṟikkoṇṭamaikku Inta nilattil mutalāka mutal koṇṭu canti
rātittavaraiyum tammuṭaiya Upayam Āka naṭattip potakkaṭavom Ākavum U Ippaṭikku Ivai vetavaṉappir(ā)mārāyaṉ Eḻuttu Ivai
tirumaṟaikkāṭupaṭṭaṉ Eḻuttu Ivai koyil nāyakam vetavaṉanampi Eḻuttu māṭāpattiyam takaṭṭūr kiḻavar veṇāvuṭaiyār Eḻuttu
Ivai māṭāpattiyam takaṭṭūr kiḻavar vaḷavataraiyaṉ Eḻuttu Ivai tirumāḷikaikkūṟu takaṭṭūr kiḻavar villavataraiyaṉ Eḻuttu .
Digital edition of SII 17.531 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
